derived.causal_summary: Remediated three deliberate Tier-2 backlog items across the analytics + learning subsystems, one per defect class. (1) Privacy: consumption_log.query_text stored raw agent-prompt text (241 live rows); the column was changed to query_hash storing a truncate+hash fingerprint (first 8 chars + ':' + 16 hex chars of sha256) computed in-process before any SQL, plus a lazy idempotent migration (PRAGMA table_info detects the old query_text column -> DROP TABLE -> recreate with query_hash; the disposable rows make the drop the PII elimination); the write path and all readers (getConsumptionPatterns group-by + intent-mismatch path key on query_hash; examples emit fingerprint: strings) updated; the 3 handlers (memory-context, memory-search, memory-triggers) rename query_text -> query; session_id left as-is (internal derived id, not PII); 45 tests pass incl. 9 new privacy tests; the live 241 PII rows are eliminated when the daemon recycles and runs the migration. (2) Dead-path: the live cost path already used the hardcoded SEEDED_MODEL_PRICING_ROWS constant; the parallel model_pricing_versioned DB table was read only by listModelPricing() which had zero non-test callers, so the table CREATE, seed INSERT, listModelPricing + row interface, and test references were deleted (75 deletions) and the constant documented as the single source of family-level pricing; 3/3 tests pass. (3) Correctness: the batch-learning boost formula divided by event count (per-event average) so the Math.min cap never fired and signal volume was ignored (3 vs 300 sessions -> identical boost); SCORE_NORMALIZATION = 10.0 was added (named, documented, tunable: ~10 strong-equivalent signals saturate the 0.10 cap) and the formula changed to min((weightedScore/10)*0.10, 0.10) so boost scales with volume; shadow-only (writes batch_learning_log.computed_boost for dashboards, does not mutate live ranking); 55 tests pass. Affected TS typechecks clean.
